content_markdown: In the $P-V$ diagram below, which line could represent an adiabatic change for an ideal gas?

diagram_metadata.description: A pressure-volume (P-V) graph showing four different process paths starting from a single point. Path A is horizontal (isobaric), Path D is vertical (isochoric), and Paths B and C are curves sloping downwards to the right. Path C is steeper than Path B.

vibe_explanation: In a P-V diagram, an adiabatic process ($PV^\gamma = \text{constant}$) is steeper than an isothermal process ($PV = \text{constant}$) because $\gamma > 1$. Line A is isobaric, Line D is isochoric, and between the two expansion curves B and C, the steeper one (C) represents the adiabatic change.
